Title: Unconventionally secreted effectors of two filamentous pathogens target plant salicylate biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for plant defense against V. dahliae
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Depletes SA, making Arabidopsis more susceptible to V. dahliae and masking VdIsc1-dependent virulence differences
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis NahG transformants with extremely low SA are more susceptible to V. dahliae than wild type. 2. In the NahG background, differences among V. dahliae WT, VdIsc1 deletion, and complementation strains are largely abolished. 3. This supports that VdIsc1 virulence function depends on suppression of host SA-mediated immunity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic Arabidopsis infection assay comparing WT and NahG plants with V. dahliae strains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in NahG transgenic Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Extremely low SA is associated with increased susceptibility to V. dahliae
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that lowering SA compromises resistance; not a desirable target for resistance breeding except as an experimental tool
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core comparison gene because it is experimentally used in Results to establish SA dependence of the pathogen ISC phenotype

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Glycine max; Gossypium hirsutum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Isochorismate branch of salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Competing side-route acting on SA precursor; downstream of chorismate-to-isochorismate formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses isochorismate to produce DDHB, thereby depleting the precursor pool for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by consuming the direct precursor of salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central signaling molecule for plant innate immunity, including PTI, ETI and systemic acquired resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Infected soybean seedlings; infected cotton roots; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces host SA and SAG accumulation and suppresses PR1 expression, thereby promoting pathogen virulence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsIsc1 is highly induced during infection and is required for full P. sojae virulence. 2. Silencing PsIsc1 increases soybean SA/SAG and PR1, while overexpression decreases SA/SAG and increases virulence. 3. PsIsc1 complements VdIsc1 deletion in V. dahliae, indicating conserved virulence function. 4. Purified PsIsc1 shows isochorismatase activity and increases DDHB accumulation in planta. 5. Catalytic triad mutations abolish enzymatic activity and defense suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene silencing and overexpression in P. sojae; complementation in V. dahliae; transient expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling of SA/SAG and DDHB; PR1 qRT-PCR; in vitro enzyme assay; catalytic-site mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; pathogen-delivered during soybean infection
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted; accumulates in haustoria and extrahaustorial matrix during infection; detected in plant cytoplasm and nucleus upon transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functions as a virulence-associated branch-diverting enzyme acting on the direct SA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection; transient Agrobacterium expression; P. capsici challenge; SA pretreatment tested in cotton context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Aspergillus nidulans trpC promoter in V. dahliae complementation constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential disease-control target; inhibition of pathogen ISC activity or compensation by SA/defense activators may improve resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lacks a classical signal peptide and is unconventionally secreted; N-terminus is required for virulence in pathogen context but dispensable for activity when directly expressed in plant cells

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Gossypium hirsutum; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Isochorismate branch of salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Competing side-route acting on SA precursor; downstream of chorismate-to-isochorismate formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses isochorismate to produce DDHB, thereby depleting the precursor pool for salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by consuming the direct precursor of salicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central signaling molecule for plant innate immunity and resistance to pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cotton roots; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; Arabidopsis context inferred through NahG assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces host SA and SAG accumulation and suppresses PR1 expression, thereby promoting full virulence of V. dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VdIsc1 is induced during infection and deletion mutants show reduced virulence on cotton. 2. VdIsc1 deletion increases cotton SA/SAG and PR1, and complementation restores virulence and metabolite levels. 3. PsIsc1 can functionally replace VdIsc1 in V. dahliae. 4. VdIsc1 suppresses defense and lowers SA/SAG when transiently expressed in N. benthamiana. 5. Purified VdIsc1 displays isochorismatase activity; catalytic triad mutations abolish activity and virulence function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion and complementation in V. dahliae; cross-species complementation with PsIsc1; transient expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling of SA/SAG and DDHB; PR1 qRT-PCR; enzyme assay; catalytic-site mutagenesis; infection assays on cotton and Arabidopsis/NahG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; pathogen-delivered during cotton and Arabidopsis infection
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted; detected in plant cytoplasm and nucleus upon transient expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functions as a virulence-associated branch-diverting enzyme acting on the direct SA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection; SA pretreatment in cotton; Arabidopsis NahG background; transient Agrobacterium expression; P. capsici challenge
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Aspergillus nidulans trpC promoter in complementation constructs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by blocking pathogen ISC activity or bolstering host SA-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lacks a classical signal peptide and is unconventionally secreted; N-terminus is required for virulence in pathogen context but dispensable for activity when directly expressed in plant cells
